capabilities.name: Sign up new user with email and password
capabilities.description: Frontend signup form collects email, password, and full name; backend proxies to FusionAuth's POST /api/user/registration (no path arg) to create a new user with the 'user' role on the primary application. On success the user is either auto-logged-in (token issued/stored) or redirected to the login page.

inputs.name: password
constraints: must include at least one digit
must include at least one symbol
never echoed in any response body
inputs.description: Plaintext password sent over TLS to backend, forwarded once to FusionAuth, never persisted locally.

inputs.name: full_name
inputs.description: Display name used in the authenticated header.

outputs.name: email
outputs.description: Echoed email of the created account.

outputs.name: next
outputs.description: Hints the frontend whether a token was returned or the user should be redirected to login.

validation_rules: Email must match RFC 5322 syntax client-side AND server-side before forwarding to FusionAuth
Password must meet FusionAuth policy (8+ chars, mixed case, digit, symbol) — surface field-level errors when FusionAuth returns fieldErrors.user.password
Reject requests where email or password is missing/empty/whitespace-only before contacting FusionAuth
Always assign role exactly ['user'] for self-signup — client-supplied role is ignored
applicationId must be the primary app id (85a03867-dccf-4882-adde-1a79aeec50df) — never accepted from client
error_cases: Missing required field (email or password) → 400 with field-level error envelope
Malformed email → 400 with field 'email' marked invalid
Weak password rejected by FusionAuth → 400 with field 'password' and FusionAuth's fieldErrors echoed
Duplicate email (FusionAuth returns [duplicate] fieldError) → 409 with field 'email' marked taken
FusionAuth unreachable / 5xx → 503 with generic 'identity provider unavailable'
Network timeout to FusionAuth (>10s) → 504
Backend missing FUSIONAUTH_API_KEY env var → 500, log clear startup-config error
Client attempts to pass role='admin' → role is silently overridden to 'user', no privilege escalation
edge_cases: Email with uppercase characters — normalize to lowercase before lookup
Email containing '+alias' — must be allowed
Unicode in fullName (emoji, CJK characters, accented chars)
Trailing/leading whitespace in email or fullName
Concurrent signup with same email — second request must get 409, not race-condition 500
Very long full_name (>100 chars) — reject with 400 before forwarding
Password exactly 8 chars meeting all classes — must succeed
Empty fullName explicitly provided — treat as not provided
FusionAuth returns 200 but without user.id (defensive) — treat as 502
test_scenarios: happy_path: new email + Password123! + name 'Alex Cook' returns 200 with user_id; user can immediately log in
duplicate_email: signing up with user@example.com returns 409 with field 'email'
weak_password: 'password' (no caps/digit/symbol) returns 400 with field 'password'
malformed_email: 'not-an-email' returns 400 with field 'email'
missing_password: empty password returns 400
role_escalation_attempt: client posts roles=['admin'], result account has only ['user']
fusionauth_down: stub 502 from auth, backend returns 503
unicode_name: fullName='Renée 👩🍳' is accepted and round-trips through /me

capabilities.name: Log in with email and password
capabilities.description: Frontend login form submits credentials to backend (or directly to FusionAuth via proxy); backend posts to FusionAuth /api/login and returns a JWT plus user identity. Token is stored client-side for use on subsequent protected requests.

inputs.name: password
inputs.description: Plaintext password forwarded once to FusionAuth.

outputs.name: access_token
outputs.description: JWT bearer token used on protected requests.

outputs.name: refresh_token
outputs.description: Refresh token used to obtain a new access token without re-login. Omitted if FusionAuth refresh tokens are not enabled — frontend then relies on re-login for session continuity.

outputs.name: user
outputs.description: Minimal identity payload for immediate UI rendering.

validation_rules: Both email and password required, non-empty after trim
applicationId injected server-side as the primary app id — never trusted from client
Backend never persists the password
Token returned to client must be the unmodified JWT from FusionAuth
error_cases: Missing email or password → 400
Invalid credentials (FusionAuth 404/200 with non-2xx response code) → 401 with generic 'invalid email or password' (do NOT leak whether email exists)
Locked/disabled account (FusionAuth 423/410) → 403 with generic 'account unavailable'
User not registered for primary application → 401 (same generic message — do not enumerate)
FusionAuth unreachable → 503
Timeout (>10s) → 504
Rate limit exceeded (if implemented) → 429 with Retry-After
edge_cases: Email submitted with different case than stored — must match (FusionAuth is case-insensitive on loginId)
Whitespace-padded email — trim before sending
Repeated rapid failed logins from same IP — log and consider rate limit
Successful login while another session is active — both should remain valid (no forced single-session)
Clock skew between backend and FusionAuth — accept default leeway (60s) when validating JWT
Admin user logging in — token must include roles=['admin'] (verify via /me)
test_scenarios: happy_path_user: user@example.com / password returns 200 with JWT whose roles contains 'user'
happy_path_admin: admin@example.com / password returns 200 with JWT whose roles contains 'admin'
bad_password: returns 401 with generic message and no JWT
unknown_email: returns 401 with same generic message (no enumeration)
missing_field: empty password → 400
fusionauth_unreachable: stubbed 502 → backend returns 503
jwt_round_trip: token returned from /login successfully validates against /me

capabilities.name: Log out current session
capabilities.description: Clears the client-side token(s) and (if refresh tokens are used) revokes them with FusionAuth. The browser is returned to the login page, and subsequent attempts to reach protected pages redirect to login.

inputs.name: refresh_token
inputs.description: If present, will be revoked via FusionAuth's logout/revoke endpoint.

outputs.name: status
outputs.description: Confirmation that the session has been cleared.

validation_rules: Endpoint is idempotent — calling logout when already logged out returns 200
Frontend MUST clear all stored tokens regardless of backend response
Logout should not require a valid JWT — accept the call even if access token is expired
error_cases: FusionAuth revoke fails → still return 200 to client (best-effort revocation), log the failure server-side
Malformed refresh_token → 200 with status='logged_out' (no information disclosure)
edge_cases: Token already expired — logout must still succeed
Multiple browser tabs — logging out in one tab should propagate (frontend listens to storage events) so other tabs also redirect to login
Logout while offline — frontend must still clear local tokens
Race: logout submitted twice — both return 200
test_scenarios: happy_path: authenticated user logs out, token storage is cleared, GET /my-recipes redirects to /login
double_logout: calling logout twice both return 200
logout_with_expired_token: succeeds without error
cross_tab_logout: tab A logs out, tab B detects and redirects to /login on next navigation

capabilities.name: GET /me — return identity of caller from JWT
capabilities.description: Protected backend endpoint. Reads the Bearer JWT, validates it (RS256, JWKS, issuer=acme.com, audience=primary app id), and returns the user's id, email, full name, and roles array extracted from the validated claims.

inputs.name: Authorization
constraints: JWT signed RS256 with kid present in JWKS
inputs.description: JWT bearer token issued by FusionAuth.

outputs.name: full_name
constraints: null when not set on profile

outputs.name: roles
outputs.description: Roles for the primary application from the JWT.

validation_rules: JWT signature verified against JWKS fetched from http://auth:9011/.well-known/jwks.json (cached, with periodic refresh — default 10 min — and on kid miss)
issuer claim MUST equal 'acme.com'
audience claim MUST equal '85a03867-dccf-4882-adde-1a79aeec50df'
alg MUST be RS256 — reject 'none' and HS*
exp claim MUST be in the future (allow default 60s leeway)
User identity is read from JWT claims — NEVER from request body or query params
error_cases: Missing Authorization header → 401 with WWW-Authenticate: Bearer
Authorization header not starting with 'Bearer ' → 401
Malformed JWT (not three segments) → 401
Signature mismatch → 401
Expired token (exp in past) → 401 with error='token_expired'
Wrong issuer → 401
Wrong audience → 401
alg=none or HS256 token → 401 (defense against algorithm confusion)
kid not found in JWKS, even after refresh → 401
JWKS endpoint unreachable on cold start → 503
edge_cases: Token validates but has empty roles array — return 200 with roles=[] (no implicit role)
Token has roles=['super_admin'] — return as-is; treat as having admin-level access where roles permit
Concurrent requests during JWKS rotation — must accept old and new kids during rotation window
Very long JWT (large claims) — accept up to 8 KB
Clock skew slightly negative — within 60s leeway accept
Trailing whitespace in Authorization header — reject (strict parsing)
test_scenarios: happy_path_user: valid user JWT returns 200 with email='user@example.com' and 'user' in roles
happy_path_admin: valid admin JWT returns 200 with 'admin' in roles
missing_auth_header: 401
expired_token: 401
wrong_audience: token issued for a different app → 401
alg_none_attack: token with alg=none → 401
hs256_attack: token signed HS256 with public key as secret → 401
tampered_signature: 401
jwks_rotation: token signed with previous kid validates during rotation window

capabilities.name: Refresh access token / persist session across page reload
capabilities.description: On page load, the frontend determines whether a valid session exists. If a refresh token is available, the frontend (or backend) exchanges it with FusionAuth for a new access token. If only an access token is available and not expired, it is reused. If neither, the user is redirected to login.

inputs.name: refresh_token
constraints: opaque string previously issued by FusionAuth

outputs.name: refresh_token
outputs.description: New refresh token if FusionAuth issues one (rotation).

validation_rules: Refresh token MUST be sent over TLS
Backend must not echo refresh tokens in logs
Rotated refresh token (if FusionAuth issues a new one) MUST replace the stored one
error_cases: Missing refresh_token → 401
Refresh token invalid/expired/revoked → 401 with error='refresh_failed' so frontend can redirect to /login
FusionAuth unreachable → 503
Network timeout → 504
edge_cases: Browser reload immediately after login — refresh token is present, session continues without re-auth
Refresh token reuse after rotation (FusionAuth flags reuse) — treat all sessions for that user as compromised: redirect to login and surface a generic 'please log in again'
Refresh while another tab is also refreshing — frontend should serialize refresh attempts (e.g., single-flight) to avoid double-spend of one-time refresh tokens
Clock skew on the client — do not rely on client clock to decide if access token is expired; let the backend respond 401 and trigger refresh
Long-idle session — refresh fails after FusionAuth's configured refresh-token TTL; user must log in again
test_scenarios: happy_path: reload page while logged in, new access token issued, user stays on /my-recipes
expired_refresh: refresh token past TTL → 401 → redirected to /login
rotated_refresh: old refresh token now invalid; new one works exactly once
concurrent_refresh: two simultaneous refresh attempts from same client do not both succeed; second waits for first

capabilities.name: Render authenticated 'My Recipes' shell
capabilities.description: Frontend route /my-recipes renders the authenticated landing page. Shows: header with app name, the user's display name (or email if no name), and a logout button. Body shows an empty-state message ('No recipes yet') since recipe features do not exist in this milestone.

inputs.name: session
constraints: valid in-memory access token + identity from /me
inputs.description: Authenticated session state held by the frontend.

outputs.name: page
constraints: header includes app brand and user name/email

validation_rules: If session is missing or invalid, MUST redirect to /login before rendering
Display name fallback order: full_name → email → 'Cook'
Empty-state copy must clearly state there are no recipes yet (no fake content)
Header must show that the user is signed in (e.g., name + logout)
error_cases: Initial /me call returns 401 → clear session, redirect to /login
Initial /me call returns 5xx → show retry UI ('Could not load your account, try again'), do not crash
Network error fetching /me → show retry UI
edge_cases: Very long full_name — truncate in header with ellipsis, full name visible via hover/title
User with no full_name set — show email
User with both 'user' and 'admin' roles (or only 'admin') — page still renders; admin lands on same shell since no admin UI exists yet
Slow /me response — show skeleton, not blank screen
Token expires while on the page — next protected fetch triggers refresh; if refresh fails, redirect to /login
test_scenarios: happy_path_user: logged-in user visits /my-recipes, sees name and empty-state
happy_path_admin: admin visits /my-recipes, sees admin name and empty-state
unauthenticated_redirect: visiting /my-recipes with no token redirects to /login
refresh_persists: reload on /my-recipes keeps user on /my-recipes
logout_from_shell: clicking logout clears session and redirects to /login
fallback_display: user without full_name shows their email in header
me_5xx: backend /me returns 500, page shows retry UI not blank screen

capabilities.name: Route guard — redirect unauthenticated visitors to login
capabilities.description: Frontend route-guard component. For any protected route (e.g., /my-recipes), if no valid session is present and a refresh attempt did not produce one, redirect to /login. Preserves the originally requested path so post-login redirect lands there.

inputs.name: target_path
inputs.description: The protected route the user attempted to load.

validation_rules: While session is being initialized (e.g., refresh in progress) show a loading state — do NOT flash-redirect to /login
After login completes, redirect to ?next path if it is a same-origin, relative path; otherwise default to /my-recipes
?next must be URL-decoded and validated to prevent open-redirect attacks (must start with '/' and not '//')
error_cases: Refresh attempt in flight — render loading, do not redirect prematurely
Refresh fails → redirect to /login
Malicious ?next='https://evil.example' → ignore, default to /my-recipes
edge_cases: Token present but expired on first paint — guard awaits refresh before deciding
Browser back button to a protected page after logout — guard catches it and redirects
Deep link (e.g., /my-recipes/anything) — preserved in ?next; if route doesn't exist post-login, fall back to /my-recipes
Open-redirect attempt via ?next=//evil.com — must be rejected
test_scenarios: unauthenticated_visit: GET /my-recipes (no token) → redirects to /login?next=%2Fmy-recipes
post_login_redirect: log in from /login?next=%2Fmy-recipes lands user on /my-recipes
open_redirect_blocked: /login?next=https%3A%2F%2Fevil.com → after login lands on /my-recipes, not evil
back_button_after_logout: after logout, browser-back to /my-recipes redirects to /login
loading_no_flash: while refresh is in flight, no flash redirect to /login

capabilities.name: Backend JWT validation dependency
capabilities.description: FastAPI dependency injected into every protected route. Extracts the Bearer token, validates RS256 signature via cached JWKS, checks issuer/audience/exp, extracts {sub, email, roles, full_name} and attaches them to the request scope as the current user.

outputs.name: current_user
outputs.description: Identity attached to request scope; consumed by route handlers.

validation_rules: JWKS fetched once at startup AND lazily on kid miss
JWKS cache TTL must default to ~10 minutes; refresh on kid miss
All algorithm allowlists must be exactly ['RS256']
Audience MUST equal the primary application id
Issuer MUST equal 'acme.com'
exp/nbf/iat are honored with default 60s leeway
Roles are taken from the JWT claim and are NEVER mutated server-side
error_cases: Missing header → 401 with body {error:'unauthenticated'}
Malformed token → 401
Invalid signature → 401
Expired → 401 {error:'token_expired'}
Wrong iss/aud → 401
alg=none / HS256 attempt → 401 (algorithm confusion defense)
JWKS fetch fails on cold start → 503 with body {error:'auth_unavailable'}
edge_cases: Token with extra unknown claims — accept, ignore the extras
Roles claim missing — treat as empty array, not as 'user'
Capitalization of 'Bearer' — case-insensitive scheme allowed
Multiple Authorization headers — reject 400
JWKS endpoint temporarily slow — block at most 2s, then return 503
test_scenarios: valid_user_token_passes: dependency yields current_user with role 'user'
valid_admin_token_passes: yields role 'admin'
missing_header_rejects: 401
alg_none_rejects: 401
wrong_audience_rejects: 401
expired_token_rejects: 401 with token_expired error
jwks_cached_across_calls: only one network fetch over 100 sequential validations

capabilities.name: Role-based authorization helper
capabilities.description: Reusable backend helper (FastAPI dependency) that, given the current_user from JWT validation, enforces that the caller holds one of the required roles. Used to lock down admin-only routes in later milestones; in this milestone only /me uses the auth dependency, but the helper must be in place and tested.

inputs.name: required_roles
inputs.description: Roles permitted to call the wrapped route.

validation_rules: Caller must hold at least one of required_roles (intersection non-empty)
'super_admin' implicitly satisfies 'admin' checks
Role names must match exactly (case-sensitive) to FusionAuth role names
Admins MUST NOT be granted implicit 'user' role unless they actually have it — moderators are not owners
error_cases: current_user has no overlapping role → 403 {error:'forbidden'}
current_user missing (somehow reached the helper without auth) → 401 (defensive)
edge_cases: User with both 'user' and 'admin' — passes either check
Empty required_roles list — programmer error, raise at import/registration time, not at request time
Role list arrives uppercase from JWT — reject; only canonical lowercase from auth contract
test_scenarios: user_can_access_user_route: required=['user'] with user token → 200
user_blocked_from_admin: required=['admin'] with user token → 403
admin_passes_admin: required=['admin'] with admin token → 200
super_admin_passes_admin: super_admin satisfies admin requirement
no_overlap_forbidden: required=['admin'] with token roles=['user'] → 403

capabilities.name: Frontend token storage and lifecycle
capabilities.description: Frontend module responsible for storing the access token (and refresh token if used), attaching it as Authorization: Bearer on outgoing requests, refreshing on 401, and clearing on logout. Tokens persist across reload so the user remains signed in.

outputs.name: authorized_fetch
outputs.description: Wrapper used by all data calls.

validation_rules: Refresh token MUST NOT be stored in document-accessible localStorage unless mitigations are present (httpOnly cookie preferred); access token MAY live in memory + a survivable store for reload
On 401 from a protected call, attempt refresh once; if refresh succeeds, retry original request once; if not, clear session and redirect to /login
Single-flight: concurrent 401s during refresh share one refresh attempt
Storage changes (logout in other tab) must trigger session clear in this tab
Never log token contents
error_cases: Refresh returns 401 → clear all tokens, redirect to /login
Refresh network error → leave existing access token in place if still valid; on next 401 retry refresh
Storage quota exceeded → fall back to in-memory only, user must re-login after reload
Storage disabled (private mode strict) → in-memory only, document the limitation
edge_cases: Two tabs, one logs out — other tab detects via storage event and redirects to /login on next interaction
Two tabs, both 401 simultaneously — only one refresh fires
Page reload mid-refresh — on next load, repeat refresh from stored refresh token
Access token close to expiry — proactive refresh is OPTIONAL; reactive 401-then-refresh must work
XSS attempt reads token — outside scope of this module but tokens must not be embedded in DOM or logged
test_scenarios: attach_bearer: authorized_fetch adds Authorization: Bearer <token>
refresh_on_401: 401 from /me triggers refresh and retry; second call succeeds
logout_clears_storage: after logout, no token remains in storage
single_flight_refresh: two simultaneous 401s cause only one /refresh call
reload_persists: token survives a full page reload
cross_tab_logout: logout in tab A triggers session clear in tab B

capabilities.name: Provision admin test user
capabilities.description: Operational capability: ensure admin@example.com exists in FusionAuth with the 'admin' role on the primary application so that admin login can be exercised in this milestone. Idempotent — safe to run repeatedly.

inputs.name: email
inputs.description: Admin login email per auth contract.

inputs.name: password
constraints: literal 'password' for dev per auth contract

outputs.name: user_id
outputs.description: Created or existing FusionAuth user id.

validation_rules: Use POST /api/user/registration (no path arg) when creating
Use PATCH /api/user/registration/{userId} to update roles on an existing user
Header: Authorization: <FUSIONAUTH_API_KEY> (raw key, NOT 'Bearer ...')
Idempotent: existing admin user must not be duplicated or have password reset
error_cases: Duplicate registration error from FusionAuth → treat as success, fetch user_id and ensure roles include 'admin'
FusionAuth unreachable → fail provisioning loudly so the deployment doesn't proceed
Missing FUSIONAUTH_API_KEY → fail with clear error
edge_cases: Admin user already exists with only 'user' role — PATCH to add 'admin'
Admin user exists with extra roles — preserve them, just ensure 'admin' is included
Repeated provisioning runs — must be a no-op after first success
test_scenarios: first_run_creates: admin user does not exist, provisioning creates it with role 'admin'
rerun_is_noop: second run does not error and does not duplicate the user
existing_user_gets_role: admin user existed with role 'user'; provisioning ensures 'admin' is added without removing 'user'
admin_can_login: after provisioning, POST /api/login with admin creds returns a JWT containing 'admin' in roles

error_envelope: All backend errors return JSON shape: {"error": "<machine_code>", "message": "<human readable>", "fields": {<optional field-level errors>}}
HTTP status reflects the failure class — do not return 200 with an error body
logging: Log structured records with request_id, user_id (when known), route, status, duration_ms
NEVER log passwords, full JWTs, refresh tokens, or Authorization headers (redact)
Log auth failures with reason code (expired, bad_sig, wrong_aud) for ops visibility
Use INFO for auth success, WARN for 4xx auth failures, ERROR for 5xx and FusionAuth outages
cors: Backend CORS allows the frontend origin (http://localhost:5173 in dev) with credentials and Authorization header
Allowed methods: GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS
Preflight OPTIONS must succeed without auth
transport: All inter-service auth flows require TLS in production; localhost http permitted only in dev
Tokens never appear in URLs or query parameters
Backend reads FUSIONAUTH_API_KEY from env, fails fast at startup if missing
jwks_handling: Cache JWKS in-process; refresh every 10 minutes and on kid miss
Hard timeout on JWKS HTTP fetch: 2 seconds
On JWKS fetch failure with stale cache present: continue using stale cache and log WARN
rate_limiting: Login endpoint should be rate-limited (e.g., 10 attempts/min/IP) to slow brute force — soft requirement, may be deferred but documented
Signup endpoint should be rate-limited per IP (e.g., 5/min) to slow account spam
audit: Login successes and failures emit an audit log entry with email (not password), IP, user-agent, and outcome
Signup successes emit an audit entry with user_id and email
Logout emits an audit entry with user_id when known
i18n_copy: Empty-state copy on /my-recipes: 'No recipes yet — once milestone two lands, you can save your first recipe here.' or equivalent (must clearly communicate empty state, not 'loading')
Generic auth failure copy: 'Invalid email or password.' (never reveal which is wrong)
session_continuity: Reloading any authenticated page MUST attempt session restore via stored tokens before deciding to redirect to /login
A loading state is shown during session restore — no flash of unauthenticated content (FOUC)
config: All FusionAuth coordinates (host, app id, issuer) sourced from env or a single config module — never hardcoded scattered
Backend env: FUSIONAUTH_URL, FUSIONAUTH_APP_ID, FUSIONAUTH_ISSUER, FUSIONAUTH_API_KEY
Frontend env: VITE_FUSIONAUTH_URL or VITE_API_BASE (whichever is used) — set per environment

anti_patterns: Never store plaintext passwords in the backend or frontend; backend forwards once to FusionAuth and discards
Never log JWTs, refresh tokens, Authorization headers, or password fields — redact at logger level
Never trust client-supplied user_id, email, or roles — always derive from the validated JWT 'sub' / 'roles' claims
Never accept a JWT with alg=none or any non-RS256 algorithm — pin algorithms=['RS256']
Never skip audience and issuer validation — both are required even if signature is valid
Never call POST /api/user/registration/{userId} to create a new user; use POST /api/user/registration with no path arg
Never send the FusionAuth API key as 'Bearer <key>' — it must be the raw key in the Authorization header
Never expose the FusionAuth API key to the frontend or browser
Never use localStorage for refresh tokens without explicit risk acceptance; prefer httpOnly cookie or in-memory + short access TTL
Never reveal whether an email exists during login failures — use a single generic 'invalid email or password' message
Never put tokens in URLs, query strings, or referer-leaking locations
Never auto-promote users to 'admin' from client-supplied input; admin role is provisioned via the privileged backend path only
Never assume a user with role 'admin' also has role 'user' — moderators are not owners; check roles explicitly
Never use the role names 'user'/'admin'/'super_admin' with different casing or synonyms (e.g., 'User', 'ADMIN', 'moderator') — exact match to FusionAuth roles
Never block UI indefinitely on JWKS fetch — short timeout with cached fallback
Never flash-redirect to /login during session restore on reload — show loading until refresh attempt resolves
Never follow ?next= values that are not same-origin relative paths — open-redirect risk
Never reuse a rotated refresh token — treat reuse as session compromise
